message.content:
You are verifier #2, independent. Do NOT optimistically confirm. Read the ACTUAL code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and independently determine whether this is a TRUE production-readiness problem in the CURRENT code (3 hardening passes already applied).

Finding G-021: build_baseline_artifact lets hash_value raise raw ValueError/TypeError on non-finite or unhashable extra_inputs
File: src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py:140
Description: At determinism.py:140 input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value) is called for every extra_input. hash_value -> canonical_json uses allow_nan=False and canonical_plain refuses non-JSON-native leaves, so a NaN/Inf float raises a bare ValueError ('Out of range float values are not JSON compliant') and an unhashable leaf raises a bare TypeError. preregistration.canonical_hypothesis_digest (preregistration.py:120-127) deliberately catches these and re-raises a typed, contextual PreRegistrationError naming the offending input; build_baseline_artifact — the other public consumer of the same canonical hashing — does not, so the determinism harness surfaces a low-context raw exception that does not identify which extra_input was bad.
PoC: build_baseline_artifact(corpus, {}, extra_inputs={'cfg': {'thr': float('nan')}}) -> ValueError: Out of range float values are not JSON compliant (no indication extra_input 'cfg' is the culprit).
Suggested fix: Wrap per-input hashing: for name, value in (extra_inputs or {}).items(): try: input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value) except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc: raise ValueError(f"extra_input {name!r} cannot be canonicalized for a reproducible hash ({exc}); use JSON-native/finite values") from None.

Confirmed=true ONLY if the problem exists as described in current code, the PoC is reproducible, and it is a genuine concern (not speculative, not already-handled, not intended design). Be concrete and cite file:line.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

100	    corpus: Corpus,
101	    contents: Mapping[str, Any],
102	    *,
103	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
104	    extra_inputs: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
105	    seed: int | None = None,
106	    corpus_hash: str | None = None,
107	    trust_corpus_hash: bool = False,
108	) -> BaselineArtifact:
109	    """Assemble a :class:`BaselineArtifact` with a complete determinism record.
110	
111	    ``contents`` is the builder's output (embeddings, similarity graph, clusters, …).
112	    ``component_versions`` records the builder + any model/library versions (the engine and
113	    Python versions are added automatically). ``extra_inputs`` are additional inputs to hash
114	    (e.g. config). ``seed``, if omitted, is derived deterministically from the corpus + inputs
115	    so it is reproducible.
116	
117	    ``corpus_hash`` lets a caller pass an already-computed :func:`corpus_fingerprint` (#119). By
118	    default it is **verified** against the corpus and a mismatch raises ``ValueError`` — a
119	    stale/typo'd hash would otherwise produce a determinism record whose fingerprint and derived
120	    seed describe a *different* corpus than the one passed, silently defeating the reproducibility
121	    guarantee this harness exists to provide (#155). Pass ``trust_corpus_hash=True`` to skip the
122	    recompute (the #119 optimization) only when the caller computed the hash from this exact
123	    corpus and accepts integrity responsibility.
124	    """
125	    if corpus_hash is None:
126	        corpus_hash = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
127	    elif not trust_corpus_hash:
128	        # Verify the supplied hash binds the corpus actually passed (default-safe, #155).
129	        actual = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
130	        if corpus_hash != actual:
131	            raise ValueError(
132	                f"corpus_hash {corpus_hash!r} does not match the corpus "
133	                f"(corpus_fingerprint={actual!r}) — the determinism record would bind the wrong "
134	                "data; pass trust_corpus_hash=True only if you accept integrity responsibility"
135	            )
136	    if extra_inputs and "corpus" in extra_inputs:
137	        raise ValueError("'corpus' is a reserved input-hash key — rename the extra input (#B7)")
138	    input_hashes = {"corpus": corpus_hash}
139	    for name, value in (extra_inputs or {}).items():
140	        input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value)
141	
142	    versions = {"engine": _ENGINE_VERSION, "python": sys.version.split()[0]}
143	    versions.update(component_versions or {})
144	
145	    if seed is None:
146	        # Derive from all input hashes (the corpus hash is already among them under "corpus").
147	        seed = stable_seed(*(input_hashes[k] for k in sorted(input_hashes)))
148	
149	    record = DeterminismRecord(seed=seed, input_hashes=input_hashes, component_versions=versions)
150	    return BaselineArtifact(
151	        corpus_fingerprint=corpus_hash, contents=contents, determinism=record.as_dict()
152	    )
153

Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the content cannot be canonicalized reproducibly
    (a non-finite float or an unhashable leaf in ``params``) — the pre-registration entrypoints
    must surface a typed firewall error, not a bare ``ValueError``/``TypeError`` (#82).
    """
    payload = {
        "hypothesis_id": hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
        "statement": hypothesis.statement,
        "kind": hypothesis.kind.value,
        "origin": hypothesis.origin.value,
        "test_name": hypothesis.test_name,
        "decision_rule": hypothesis.decision_rule,
        "source_claim_id": hypothesis.source_claim_id,
        "predicted_direction": hypothesis.predicted_direction,
        "params": dict(hypothesis.params),
    }
    try:
        return hash_text(canonical_json(payload))
    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
        raise PreRegistrationError(
            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} cannot be canonicalized for a content "
            f"digest ({exc}); pre-registration requires reproducibly hashable content "
            "(no non-finite floats or unhashable leaves in params)"
        ) from None

content.text:
PoC fully reproduced. The full public entrypoint `build_baseline_artifact` raises a bare `ValueError` ("Out of range float values are not JSON compliant: nan") with no mention of which `extra_input` ('cfg') is the culprit. The sibling consumer of the same canonical hashing (`canonical_hypothesis_digest` at preregistration.py:120-127) deliberately wraps these into a typed, named `PreRegistrationError` per #82, establishing the project's own intended pattern that this site violates.

Assessment of validity and severity: This is a real inconsistency and a genuine ergonomics/diagnostics gap, but it is not a correctness, security, or data-integrity bug. The function still fails loudly and refuses to produce a misleading hash — it just doesn't name the offending input. Both consumers are internal-facing developer/study-construction APIs. The asymmetry with the deliberately-wrapped preregistration path makes this a legitimate finding, but the impact is low: a slightly less helpful error message on bad caller input.

input.reasoning: Verified against current code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (3 passes applied). At src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py:140, build_baseline_artifact does `input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value)` per extra_input with no per-input try/except. hash_value re-exported from assay_engine._canonical: canonical_json (_canonical.py:69-71) calls json.dumps(..., allow_nan=False), so a NaN/Inf float raises bare ValueError; canonical_plain (_canonical.py:60-65) raises bare TypeError for any non-allowlisted leaf. PoC reproduced through the actual public entrypoint: build_baseline_artifact(corpus, {}, extra_inputs={'cfg': {'thr': float('nan')}}) -> ValueError: 'Out of range float values are not JSON compliant: nan' (no indication extra_input 'cfg' is the culprit); extra_inputs={'cfg': object()} -> bare TypeError. The sibling consumer of the same canonical primitive, canonical_hypothesis_digest at src/assay_engine/methodology/preregistration.py:119-127, deliberately wraps exactly these (except (ValueError, TypeError)) into a typed, contextual PreRegistrationError naming the hypothesis (per issue #82). build_baseline_artifact does not, so the determinism harness surfaces a low-context raw exception that does not identify the offending input — a real and project-acknowledged asymmetry. Note: the finding's path 'preregistration.py:120-127' is slightly off (file lives under methodology/, handler at lines 119-127), but the substance is accurate. Genuine concern, not speculative or already-handled.

content.text: Verified and confirmed. Finding G-021 is a true (low-severity) issue: `build_baseline_artifact` at `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py:140` lets the bare `ValueError`/`TypeError` from `hash_value` (defined in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/_canonical.py`) escape without naming the offending `extra_input`, unlike the sibling `canonical_hypothesis_digest` at `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/methodology/preregistration.py:119-127` which deliberately wraps them. PoC reproduced through the real public entrypoint. Impact is limited to a less-helpful error message on bad caller input — not a correctness or integrity bug.
